questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The order of boiling points of the following compounds is
1. \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \ddot{\mathrm{N}}\)
2. \(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \ddot{\mathrm{NH}}_2\)
3. \(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \ddot{\mathrm{N}} \mathrm{HCH}_3\)
questions.solution: Like alkanes dominating inter-molecular force for association of amines to its liquid state is van der Waals' force. We know, \(\text {van der Waals' force } \propto \frac{1}{\text { branching of chain }} \propto \text { boiling point }\) Boiling point

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify $X$ and $Y$ in the following reactions
questions.solution:
(i) On reduction in neutral media, using $\mathrm{Zn}$ dust and $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ solution nitrobenzene gives phenyl hydroxylamine.
While in alkaline medium, using $\mathrm{Zn}-\mathrm{NaOH}$, mononuclear intermediate products (nitrobenzene and phenylhydroxyl amine) interact to each other to give dinuclear product. Final product using $\mathrm{Zn}-\mathrm{NaOH}$ is hydrazobenzene, which in a formed viz the formation of azoxybenzene and azobenzene.

questions.q: Reduction of nitrobenzene with $\mathrm{Zn}$ and alcoholic $\mathrm{KOH}$ solution results in the formation of the following compound

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following gives a foul-smelling substance when treated with chloroform and alcoholic $\mathrm{KOH}$ ?
questions.solution: Aliphatic and aromatic primary amines on heating with chloroform and alcoholic $\mathrm{KOH}$ form isocyanides or carbylamines which are foul smelling substances. Secondary and tertiary amines do not show this reaction. This reaction is known as carbylamine reaction or isocyanide test. Hence, the compound should be benzylamine.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
